action: Open Google Chrome on the computer. Then open a new tab and navigate to 'https://www.youtube.com' in that new tab.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open Google Chrome. On the current screen, I can see the Google Chrome web browser icon (Box ID 155) in the taskbar or app area. I should click this icon to launch Google Chrome.

previous action: I was instructed to open Google Chrome by clicking its icon (Box ID 155). Looking at the current screen, I can see the Google Chrome browser window is now open. The Chrome UI is visible, confirming the previous action succeeded. The next step is to open a new tab in Chrome. The standard way to do this is by using the Ctrl+T hotkey.

previous action: I used the Ctrl+T hotkey to open a new tab in Google Chrome. Looking at the current screen, I can see that a new tab has been opened in Chrome, as indicated by the presence of a blank tab and the Chrome UI. The next step is to navigate to 'https://www.youtube.com' in this new tab. To do this, I need to type the URL into the address bar. The address bar is typically at the top of the Chrome window, but there is no specific bounding box for it. I will click near the top center of the Chrome window to focus the address bar before typing the URL.

previous action: I was instructed to click near the top center of the Chrome window to focus the address bar. Looking at the current screen, the address bar appears to be focused, as indicated by the visual highlight and the blinking cursor in the address bar area. This means the previous action succeeded. Now, I need to type 'https://www.youtube.com' into the address bar to navigate to YouTube.
